target.title: [codex] Cron: repair empty sanitized final replies
target.body:
﻿???## Summary

- Problem: cron-owned agent runs could finish with no deliverable final text after sanitization and end up as silent `not-delivered` outcomes.
- Why it matters: scheduled reports can vanish without a clear error even though the cron job itself ran, which makes delivery failures hard to diagnose.
- What changed: add one repair pass for empty sanitized output, fail loudly when no deliverable text remains, enforce tool-disabled repair for embedded runs, and use an explicit CLI repair mode that resumes the first CLI session while disabling bundled MCP wiring.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not relax reasoning-tag sanitization, does not change normal non-cron agent turns, and does not address the legacy local WhatsApp health-check cron issue tracked separately in #60204.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Exact bug issue: none existed at PR open time
- Related #60204 for the misleading legacy WhatsApp health-check path uncovered during the same investigation
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: cron delivery could receive an empty final text after sanitization (for example when malformed reasoning tags consumed the visible reply), but the cron runner had no recovery pass and the delivery path did not convert that condition into a hard error.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the runner did not retry with a constrained no-tools finalization prompt, and the direct-delivery path treated missing synthesized text too softly.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): the existing sanitization behavior is intentional; the gap was in cron finalization and delivery handling, not in the sanitizer itself.
- Why this regressed now: stricter sanitization can legitimately collapse malformed final replies to empty text, but cron still assumed a deliverable text reply would exist.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: ruled out transport-only failure for the investigated case; the actionable gap here was the empty-output cron path.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.interim-retry.test.ts`, `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.owner-auth.test.ts`, `src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: when cron final output becomes empty after sanitization, the runner performs one repair pass; embedded runs enforce `disableTools`, CLI runs resume the first CLI session and disable bundled MCP wiring, external-hook CLI cron runs keep `senderIsOwner=false` for both normal and repair calls, and if repair still yields no deliverable text the run fails loudly instead of silently ending `not-delivered`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug lives in cron run finalization plus delivery dispatch, and the narrowest stable guardrail is to exercise those exact seams with mocked agent output.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing interim-retry coverage already covered acknowledgement retries but not empty sanitized output.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Cron jobs that lose their visible final text after sanitization now get one repair attempt before delivery.
- If no deliverable text exists after that repair attempt, the cron run records an explicit error instead of silently finishing as `not-delivered`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[cron run] -> [sanitized final text becomes empty] -> [delivery path sees no text] -> [silent not-delivered]

After:
[cron run] -> [sanitized final text becomes empty] -> [no-tools repair pass]
          -> [recovered text -> deliver] or [still empty -> explicit cron error]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: the embedded repair path enforces `disableTools`; the CLI repair path is explicitly not represented as native-tool-free, resumes the first CLI session, and disables OpenClaw-bundled MCP wiring. The risk is limited to cron finalization behavior, and dedicated regression tests cover both embedded and CLI-backed paths.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu Linux
- Runtime/container: local Node + pnpm checkout
- Model/provider: mocked embedded + CLI runner paths in targeted tests
- Integration/channel (if any): cron + Telegram announce path in regression coverage
- Relevant config (redacted): default test fixtures

### Steps

1. Run `pnpm test -- src/cron/isolated-agent/run.interim-retry.test.ts`
2. Run `pnpm test -- src/cron/isolated-agent.skips-delivery-without-whatsapp-recipient-besteffortdeliver-true.test.ts`
3. Run `pnpm test -- src/agents/cli-runner.prepare.test.ts`

### Expected

- Empty sanitized cron output triggers one repair pass: embedded runs use `disableTools`, and CLI runs resume the first CLI session with bundled MCP disabled.
- If repair still produces no final text, the run finishes with an explicit error.
- CLI-backed repair mode does not inject bundled MCP overlays.

### Actual

- All three targeted tests pass with the new repair and fail-loud behavior in place.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: reviewed the cron finalization and delivery diff, ran the targeted regression tests locally, confirmed CLI repair resumes the first CLI session, confirmed external-hook CLI cron runs preserve non-owner sender scope for normal and repair calls, and confirmed the local changed-file check stack passed.
- Edge cases checked: empty payloads without descendants, repair success, repair failure, CLI repair-mode bundled MCP disablement, and first CLI session reuse during repair.
- What you did **not** verify: live WhatsApp/Telegram delivery against a real gateway session.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Cron-owned CLI agent runs that produce no deliverable final text now perform one repair pass and record the repaired final text instead of silently ending without a user-visible result. For CLI-backed cron runs, the repair pass resumes the first CLI session and disables OpenClaw-bundled MCP wiring without claiming native CLI tools are unavailable.

Real environment tested: Windows/PowerShell checkout. The live repair proof below was run at repair head `0f1b25fbfc`; current pushed head `d6ed463c96` keeps that repair path, additionally restores external-hook CLI non-owner sender scope, includes the upstream lint repair needed for the final CI head, and addresses the Greptile duplicate-descendant-predicate review note; verified with targeted red/green tests plus `pnpm lint --threads=8`. Local OpenClaw Gateway was at `ws://127.0.0.1:19162` with isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`; registered `codex-cli` runtime used installed OpenAI Codex CLI `codex-cli 0.130.0-alpha.5` through a thin proof wrapper around `codex exec --output-last-message`; cron delivery was disabled intentionally with `--no-deliver`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
corepack pnpm openclaw gateway health --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --json

corepack pnpm openclaw cron add --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --name "PR60212 fixed repair marker proof" --at "2030-01-08T00:00:00Z" --keep-after-run --session isolated --message "Real behavior proof for PR 60212. If the prompt you are reading contains the phrase Repair it now, reply exactly OPENCLAW_CRON_REPAIR_OK. Otherwise return an empty final answer and do not output any visible text." --no-deliver --timeout-seconds 300 --model openai/gpt-5.5 --json

corepack pnpm openclaw cron run 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --expect-final --timeout 360000

corepack pnpm openclaw cron show 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --json

corepack pnpm openclaw cron runs --id 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --limit 5 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted>
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ corepack pnpm openclaw gateway health --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --json
{
  "ok": true,
  "eventLoop": { "degraded": false, "reasons": [] },
  "plugins": { "loaded": ["acpx", "browser", "canvas", "device-pair", "file-transfer", "memory-core", "openai", "phone-control", "talk-voice"], "errors": [] }
}

$ corepack pnpm openclaw cron run 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --expect-final --timeout 360000
{
  "ok": true,
  "enqueued": true,
  "runId": "manual:3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091:1778695539774:2"
}

$ corepack pnpm openclaw cron show 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --json
{
  "id": "3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091",
  "name": "PR60212 fixed repair marker proof",
  "delivery": { "mode": "none", "channel": "last" },
  "state": {
    "lastRunStatus": "ok",
    "lastStatus": "ok",
    "lastDurationMs": 14308,
    "lastDeliveryStatus": "not-requested",
    "consecutiveErrors": 0
  }
}

$ corepack pnpm openclaw cron runs --id 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --limit 5 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted>
{
  "entries": [
    {
      "jobId": "3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091",
      "action": "finished",
      "status": "ok",
      "summary": "OPENCLAW_CRON_REPAIR_OK",
      "runId": "manual:3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091:1778695539774:2",
      "durationMs": 14308,
      "model": "gpt-5.5",
      "provider": "codex-cli",
      "delivered": false,
      "deliveryStatus": "not-requested"
    }

  "hasMore": false

```

Observed result after fix: The real local gateway accepted the cron job, ran it through the registered `codex-cli` backend, recovered `OPENCLAW_CRON_REPAIR_OK` during the repair flow, and recorded the run as `status: "ok"`. Delivery was not requested by design because the proof command used `--no-deliver`; the current code keeps the CLI mode explicit, resumes the first CLI session for repair, and does not hit the earlier native-tool fail-closed error.

What was not tested: External WhatsApp/Telegram send was not exercised; delivery was disabled to keep the local proof isolated and non-invasive.
Current-head ownership, lint, and Greptile cleanup verification after commit `d6ed463c96`:

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a cron run with intentionally empty final output now incurs one extra model pass before failing.

- Risk: CLI repair mode could accidentally preserve bundled MCP wiring.

target.title: fix(openai): mark mp3 TTS voice output compatible
target.body:
Fixes #80317.

## Summary

- Fixes OpenAI-compatible TTS voice-note compatibility for configured `responseFormat: "mp3"`.
- Reuses the shared media runtime voice-compatibility contract instead of hard-coding OpenAI response formats.
- Adds provider and speech-core regressions so provider-compatible MP3 output continues to route as Telegram voice delivery.

## Problem summary

The OpenAI speech provider only marked voice-note synthesis as voice-compatible when `responseFormat === "opus"`. That made OpenAI-compatible local TTS servers configured for MP3 produce an audio file result even though the shared media contract and Telegram send path already allow MP3 voice delivery.

## Root cause

`extensions/openai/speech-provider.ts` used a provider-local equality check for `opus`, while the rest of the TTS/channel path uses `isVoiceCompatibleAudio()` to decide whether a produced audio asset can be delivered as a voice note. Microsoft/Edge TTS already follows that shared helper path for MP3 output, so OpenAI-compatible MP3 output diverged from the existing runtime contract.

## Architectural reasoning

This keeps the behavior at the provider boundary: the OpenAI provider still reports compatibility only for a `voice-note` synthesis target, but it now asks the shared media runtime whether the selected container extension is voice-compatible. The change does not alter provider selection, gateway/session flow, async cleanup, request timing, or Telegram transport behavior. It only corrects the metadata emitted by OpenAI-compatible synthesis results so the existing speech-core and Telegram delivery contracts can do what they already support.

## Testing performed

Local proof on refreshed head `7d6773b18c4679697f5ca65c9d1fa7b1ac382b5b`:

- `pnpm test extensions/openai/speech-provider.test.ts extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` passed: OpenAI provider 10/10, speech-core 28/28.
- `pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose -t "routes audio media to sendAudio/sendVoice based on voice compatibility"` passed: 1/1 selected, 102 skipped.
- Local OpenAI-compatible HTTP proof using production `buildOpenAISpeechProvider()` captured `POST /v1/audio/speech` with `response_format: "mp3"`; the returned result was `outputFormat: "mp3"`, `fileExtension: ".mp3"`, and `voiceCompatible: true`.
- `pnpm format:check -- extensions/openai/speech-provider.ts extensions/openai/speech-provider.test.ts extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`

GitHub Actions on `7d6773b18c4679697f5ca65c9d1fa7b1ac382b5b`:

- Workflow Sanity #201563: success
- OpenGrep PR Diff #21199: success
- CI #207277: success
- CodeQL Critical Quality #17155: success

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI-compatible TTS configured with `responseFormat: "mp3"` should mark voice-note synthesis output as voice-compatible so Telegram delivery can use the voice path instead of falling back to audio-file delivery.

Real environment tested: Local Windows checkout on branch `Hemant/fix-openai-tts-mp3-voice`, Node `v22.15.0` with the repository's existing engine warning. The branch was rebased onto upstream/main `6baa2b38b2712384d7de460d90b127cd78dbb3a5`; upstream moved to `bc4f27c89a3c5aa40b7fb9e507a91b6e6c753307` while checks ran, and GitHub reported the PR mergeable against the live base after push. The extra proof used the production OpenAI speech provider against a real local HTTP OpenAI-compatible speech endpoint with redacted auth.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a local HTTP server, invoked `buildOpenAISpeechProvider().synthesize()` with `baseUrl` pointed at that server, `responseFormat: "mp3"`, and `target: "voice-note"`, then captured the outbound OpenAI-compatible request and provider result.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "capturedRequest": {
    "method": "POST",
    "url": "/v1/audio/speech",
    "responseFormat": "mp3",
    "model": "local-compatible-tts",
    "voice": "alloy"
  },
  "outputFormat": "mp3",
  "fileExtension": ".mp3",
  "voiceCompatible": true,
  "audioBytes": 6
}
```

Observed result after fix: The production provider requests MP3 from the OpenAI-compatible speech endpoint and returns MP3 voice-note synthesis as voice-compatible. The speech-core regression keeps that provider-compatible MP3 result on Telegram voice delivery, and the Telegram send regression confirms compatible MP3 voice delivery maps to `sendVoice`.

What was not tested: I did not send a live Telegram message or call a credentialed third-party OpenAI-compatible TTS server. The local HTTP proof exercises the same production provider request/result path without secrets.

## Risk analysis

- Runtime behavior: Narrow metadata correction for OpenAI-compatible TTS results only.
- Concurrency/session/gateway flows: No changes.
- Cleanup/lifecycle handling: No changes.
- API behavior: No public config or SDK shape changes.
- Backward compatibility: Existing Opus voice-note behavior stays compatible; WAV remains non-voice-compatible through the shared helper.
- Developer ergonomics: Removes duplicated provider-local format policy and aligns OpenAI with the shared media contract already used by other providers.

## Backward compatibility

Backward compatible. Users who explicitly configured `responseFormat: "mp3"` for OpenAI-compatible TTS now get the expected voice-compatible metadata for voice-note targets. Non-voice targets and formats that are not accepted by the shared media helper keep their previous fallback behavior.

target.title: fix(feishu): convert presentation to card on message.edit
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When the agent calls `message.edit` with both `message` (text) and `presentation` (e.g. `{"title":"","tone":"info","blocks":[]}`), the Feishu channel's edit handler only checked `ctx.params.card` for the card payload. The agent's message tool produces the standardised `presentation` field, not the Feishu-specific `card`. With `presentation` silently ignored, the handler attempted a text-only `PATCH` on the original interactive card message. The Feishu API rejects changing an interactive card to a plain text post with error code 200621 (`parse card json err`).

The `send` action already handled `presentation` correctly by calling `normalizeMessagePresentation` + `buildFeishuPresentationCard`, but the `edit` action did not.

Fixes #93197

## Evidence

**Behavior addressed**: `message.edit` on Feishu with `presentation` containing empty `blocks: []` fails with "parse card json err" and the agent retries for ~40 seconds.

**Real setup tested**:
- Runtime: Node v24.13.1, Linux x86_64

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
$ pnpm test -- extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  64 passed (64)
```

**Observed result after the fix**: The edit action handler now calls `normalizeMessagePresentation` on `ctx.params.presentation` before falling back to raw `card`/`text`:

```
presentation = normalizeMessagePresentation(ctx.params.presentation)
presentation valid? No (empty blocks, no title) → undefined → fall back to text/card params
presentation valid? Yes (has blocks or title) → buildFeishuPresentationCard → card edit
→ Feishu API accepts card PATCH on interactive message → success
```

Before fix:
```
ctx.params.card = undefined → text-only edit path
→ Feishu API rejects text PATCH on interactive message
→ error: "Failed to create card content, ext=ErrCode: 200621; ErrMsg: parse card json err"
```

The `send` action (line 780-786) already worked this way. The `edit` action (line 873-896) was missing the same `presentation` handling. The fix mirrors the `send` action's pattern exactly.

**What was not tested**: End-to-end Feishu API call (requires real Feishu credentials and active group chat). The fix is verified through unit tests and code review.

## Tests and validation

| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests | ✅ | 64/64 passed (channel.test.ts) |
| All Feishu Tests | ✅ | 961/961 passed (71 test files) |
| TypeScript Check | ✅ | No errors |

### New tests added (Phase 10)

| Test | Description |
|------|-------------|
| edits messages with presentation blocks as card | Presentation with title + blocks → card edit call |
| edits messages falling back when presentation has empty blocks and no title | Empty presentation → text-only edit fallback |
| edits messages with title-only presentation as card | Title-only presentation → card with header |

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)
- Feishu message edits with `presentation` will now correctly produce card-type edits instead of failing
- Edits without `presentation` (text-only or raw `card` param) are unchanged

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

What is the highest-risk area?
- Feishu card edit path: the fix mirrors the existing `send` action's proven `presentation` handling pattern

How is that risk mitigated?
- The change is minimal (+9/-2 lines) and exactly mirrors the `send` action pattern
- 3 new focused tests verify both card and text fallback behavior
- All 961 feishu extension tests pass

## Current review state

What is the next action?
- Maintainer review

target.title: gateway: gate tool invoke until startup hooks are ready
target.body:
## Summary
- gate tools.invoke through the existing startup-unavailable method mechanism so tool invocation returns 503 while startup sidecars/plugins are still loading
- propagate unavailableGatewayMethods into the HTTP /tools/invoke path, covering direct HTTP in addition to RPC gateway methods
- preserve the gateway global hook runner when later scoped/default plugin runtime loads have fewer hooks, so startup-loaded security hooks remain active after readiness

## Why
OpenClaw binds the HTTP port before runtime-post-bind plugin startup completes. Before this patch, /tools/invoke could be accepted during that window while before_tool_call hooks were unavailable, causing a fail-open tool invocation path. This exists as a startup race, not a steady-state plugin failure.

## Verification
- pnpm test src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts src/gateway/server-http.request-trace.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods.control-plane-rate-limit.test.ts src/plugins/loader.test.ts src/plugins/providers.test.ts
- local live staging: fake tool race probe showed connection refused -> 503 plugins_not_ready -> 404 not_found
- local live prod after patch: fake tool race probe showed connection refused -> 503 plugins_not_ready -> 404 not_found; warm web_fetch to webhook.site returned 403 [T-EXFIL-003]

## Notes
The branch is based on current upstream main and contains only the focused readiness/hook-runner fix commits.

target.title: Discord: add reaction notification wake policy
target.body:
## Summary

- Add shared `notificationWake.reactions` policy support so notification-backed reaction events queue by default, can opt into immediate wake, or can be disabled with `off`.
- Wire Discord reaction notifications through that shared helper after existing Discord ingress authorization and routing.
- Preserve stable `reaction_key` values for standard/custom emoji and keep deduped reaction events from waking.
- Add heartbeat preflight handling so `notification-wake:*` wakes still run when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty, while generic notification events continue to honor the empty-file skip.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83164
- Related #47687
- Related #83611
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord reaction notifications now pass through a shared notification wake policy. Accepted reactions enqueue routed system events by default, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests a targeted `notification-wake:discord-reaction` heartbeat only after successful enqueue, `off` skips enqueue after ingress authorization, and deduped events do not wake.
- Real environment tested: redacted OpenClaw source checkout on PR head `31de50ea3b8e`. Earlier redacted Discord API canary proof on this PR verified the Discord bot/API reaction path and listener event shape without publishing secrets, usernames, chat titles, local paths, or raw IDs; the current head was reverified with focused policy/runtime regression tests and project checks below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
  - `pnpm config:channels:check`
  - `pnpm config:docs:check`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/discord.md extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/listeners.reactions.ts src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts src/config/types.agents.ts src/config/types.channels.ts src/config/types.discord.ts src/config/types.notifications.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts src/config/zod-schema.channels-config.ts src/config/zod-schema.notifications.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.ts src/plugin-sdk/system-event-runtime.ts`
  - `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`
  - `pnpm tsgo:prod`
  - `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
- Evidence after fix:
  Redacted terminal output from the live Discord API canary for this PR's reaction listener path:

```json
{
  "discordBotIdentity": "verified_with_getCurrentUser",
  "canaryMessagePosted": true,
  "botReactionAddedViaRest": true,
  "guildHash": "01738befea7f",
  "channelHash": "5e33a98adfea",
  "messageHash": "9464114d77b7",
  "actorHash": "ab848dd6c34a",
  "systemEventQueued": true,
  "heartbeatRequestCallPresent": true,
  "sessionKeyHash": "7fbbcb1140b0",
  "eventTextShape": "Discord reaction added: <emoji> by <actor> on <guild> <channel> msg <message-id> (reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>) from <message-author>",
  "contextKeyHash": "d99c9ca6122b",
  "forceSenderIsOwnerFalse": true,
  "listenerLogs": []
}
```

  - Focused regression tests passed: 3 files, 92 tests.
  - Generated channel config check passed.
  - Generated docs baseline check passed with `OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`.
  - Formatter reported all 21 touched files use the correct format.
  - Git whitespace checks exited cleanly.
  - Typecheck passed for core and extensions through `pnpm tsgo:prod`.
  - Plugin contracts passed: 62 files, 857 tests.
- Observed result after fix: default Discord reaction handling queues exactly one system event and does not request heartbeat; opt-in `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests `source: "notifications-event"`, `intent: "immediate"`, `reason: "notification-wake:discord-reaction"`, the routed `agentId`, and routed `sessionKey`; `off` skips enqueue; duplicate enqueue does not wake; custom emoji retain stable `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` context keys; `notification-wake:*` preflight bypasses empty `HEARTBEAT.md` skip while generic notification events do not.
- What was not tested: no production Discord Gateway restart was performed after this follow-up refactor. The prior redacted live Discord API canary on this PR covered external bot/API reaction reachability; this update adds exact current-head local runtime proof for the wake policy and heartbeat preflight behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the previous patch woke every accepted Discord reaction immediately, which made the behavior too aggressive and inconsistent with the shared notification policy in #83611.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Discord reaction notification enqueue was wired directly to heartbeat wake behavior instead of going through a configurable notification wake policy.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered reaction enqueue/routing but did not distinguish queue-by-default, opt-in wake, disabled enqueue, deduped no-wake, or empty-`HEARTBEAT.md` notification wake preflight.
- Contributing context (if known): Discord and Telegram reaction PRs were converging on similar semantics without a shared helper.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests or files:
  - `src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts`
  - `extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
- Scenario the tests lock in: shared policy resolution precedence, queue default, explicit wake, explicit off, deduped no-wake, Discord listener wiring, custom emoji stable keys, and notification wake preflight through empty `HEARTBEAT.md`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these tests exercise the shared helper and Discord listener seam directly without broad channel startup or live gateway flake.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing Discord reaction tests covered authorization/routing and enqueue shape; this PR adds the missing wake-policy assertions.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord reaction notifications that pass existing access controls are queued on the routed session by default. Operators can set `channels.discord.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` for immediate reaction-only turns or `"off"` to suppress reaction notification enqueueing after authorization. System event text keeps stable `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` and `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` markers.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Default:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued]

Opt-in wake:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued] -> [notification-wake heartbeat]

Disabled:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [notificationWake.reactions=off] -> [skip enqueue]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: redacted local development environment
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord monitor harness and shared notification/heartbeat runtime tests
- Relevant config (redacted): default reaction policy, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`, and `notificationWake.reactions: "off"`

### Steps

1. Run the focused notification, Discord monitor, and heartbeat preflight tests.
2. Run generated config/docs checks.
3. Run formatter, whitespace, typecheck, and plugin contract checks.

### Expected

- Accepted reactions queue by default.
- Explicit wake policy requests the routed notification heartbeat after enqueue.
- Disabled policy skips enqueue.
- Deduped events do not wake.
- Empty `HEARTBEAT.md` does not suppress `notification-wake:*` turns.

### Actual

- Passed as described in the proof section.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: shared policy default/override resolution, Discord queue default, opt-in wake, off skip, deduped no-wake, custom emoji stable key, heartbeat preflight for notification wake, generated config/docs consistency, typecheck, plugin contract suite.
- Edge cases checked: blocked DM/guild/group reactions remain blocked by existing tests, reaction notification modes still gate behavior, generic notification events still skip when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty.
- What you did **not** verify: production Discord Gateway delivery after this follow-up refactor.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Optional new `notificationWake.reactions` config
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operators expecting every reaction to wake immediately need an explicit opt-in.
  - Mitigation: default queue preserves visibility without surprise model wakes; `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` restores immediate processing intentionally.
- Risk: notification wake preflight could over-bypass empty `HEARTBEAT.md` gating.
  - Mitigation: the bypass is limited to `source: "notifications-event"` plus `reason` prefix `notification-wake:`; generic notification events still skip.

target.title: fix(telegram): move preview-streamed dedup to channel layer (#80520)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the Telegram "reply appears briefly then disappears" regression reported in #80520. The previous core-layer dedup (#82625 / commit `bd51d8f2dd`) suppressed final payloads matching preview-streamed text but had no path to also signal the channel's `lane.finalized` state. When every final was suppressed (e.g., embedded harness + reasoning-model paragraph-split deliveries), the lane stayed unfinalized and the end-of-turn cleanup deleted the preview message.

This PR moves the dedup from the channel-agnostic core to the Telegram channel layer so the "skip duplicate send" and "mark lane finalized" steps happen atomically.

## Commits

1. **Revert "Deduplicate preview-streamed final replies (#82625)"** — undoes the previous dedup, which could not observe channel state.

2. **fix(telegram): move preview-streamed final dedup to channel layer (#80520)** — equivalent dedup at the channel layer:
   - New `extensions/telegram/src/preview-dedup.ts` helpers (whole + per-block normalization, exact-match check). 13 unit tests in `preview-dedup.test.ts`.
   - In `bot-message-dispatch.ts`, the dispatcher's `deliver` callback checks each text-only final against the answer lane's `lastPartialText`. On match: `answerLane.finalized = true`, `deliveryState.markDelivered()`, skip the send.
   - **Guards with a `previewMessageId` check** — only fires when the preview actually has an established Telegram message. If the preview never landed (e.g., transient failure), the final still flows through `sendPayload` so the user receives something. The previous core-layer dedup had no access to this state.
   - Media + caption payloads keep their existing path through `lane-delivery-text-deliverer.ts`'s `hasMedia` branch, which already handles caption stripping.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram "reply appears briefly then disappears" regression for embedded-harness + reasoning-model + `streaming.mode=partial` paths (#80520).

Real environment tested: macOS, managed gateway service running this branch's `dist/`. Live Telegram bot configured with `model.primary = zai/glm-5.1` (reasoning model) via the embedded harness with `streaming.mode=partial`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `git checkout dev/telegram-preview-dedup && pnpm build` (build the patched `dist/`).
2. `openclaw gateway stop && openclaw gateway start` (run a live gateway on the patched build).
3. Sent a short (3-char) conversational message to the live agent through the Telegram client.
4. Sent an 80-char prompt asking the live agent to fetch and summarize an external URL; the model produced a 10-paragraph, ~1297-char markdown summary — the paragraph-split delivery shape this regression originally manifested on.
5. (Supplemental) Ran `pnpm test extensions/telegram` against the patched branch — 118 files, 1735 tests pass, including 4 new integration cases for the channel-layer dedup, the `previewMessageId` guard, multi-block deliveries, and error preservation.

Evidence after fix:

Redacted live `gateway.log` excerpt covering the 80-char inbound prompt and the subsequent long-reply turn on the live gateway (chat id and bot handle redacted as `<chat>` and `<bot>`; nothing else inside this turn was logged):

```
[telegram] Inbound message <chat> -> @<bot> (direct, 80 chars)
(no other [telegram] outbound line for this account between the inbound
 and 3 minutes later — no sendMessage, no deliverReplies, no
 editMessageTelegram from the channel adapter for this turn)
```

The absence of any outbound `[telegram]` line is the load-bearing observation from the live gateway: the 1297-char, 10-paragraph reply was delivered entirely through the existing preview-streaming `editMessageText` path on the already-established preview message id. On `main` without this PR, the previous `suppressPreviewStreamedPayloads` would have dropped each paragraph's final payload at the runner layer and `clearUnfinalizedStream` would have then deleted the preview message — the regression described in #80520.

Redacted live session jsonl excerpt for the same turn (assistant payload metadata as written by the running gateway; reply text and tool inputs/outputs omitted):

```json
{
  "type": "message",
  "message": {
    "role": "assistant",
    "provider": "zai",
    "model": "glm-5.1",
    "api": "openai-completions",
    "stopReason": "stop",
    "usage": {
      "input": 10445,
      "output": 585,
      "cacheRead": 130176,
      "totalTokens": 141206,
      "cost": { "input": 0.012534, "output": 0.00234, "cacheRead": 0.031242, "total": 0.046116 }
    },
    "content": [
      { "type": "text", "text": "<redacted: ~1297-char markdown reply with 10 paragraph blocks separated by blank lines>" }
    ]
  }
}
```

The same turn also wrote a `provider=openclaw, model=delivery-mirror` mirror entry ~11 s later with the same `content[0].text` value, confirming the channel side accepted the reply as delivered and finalized the lane — the cleanup did not fire and the preview message remained intact in the Telegram chat.

Before this PR on `main` (the parent of `56b09263ef`, before reverting `bd51d8f2dd`), the same setup repeatedly produced a preview message that flashed into the chat and disappeared without any visible final, matching the report on #80520. Reverting `bd51d8f2dd` alone restored delivery (with paragraph duplicates); this PR adds the channel-layer dedup that also removes the duplicates.

Observed result after fix:
- The 10-paragraph, ~1297-char live reply lands as a single Telegram chat bubble that contains every paragraph (not 10 separate messages) and stays in the chat permanently.
- No flashing/disappearing during or after streaming.
- No paragraph-split duplicates.
- Short conversational replies behave the same way (single message, stable).

What was not tested:
- Slack, MSTeams, Mattermost (the analogous dispatchers in those channels were not modified by this PR; whether they need similar treatment is follow-up work).
- Codex-harness paths (different code path, not affected by this PR).
- Live multi-instance / concurrent-turn scenarios.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/telegram` — 1735/1735 pass
- [x] Manual Telegram E2E with `zai/glm-5.1` embedded harness: short reply + 10-paragraph URL summary
- [ ] Maintainer to confirm whether Slack/MSTeams dispatchers need analogous treatment in a follow-up

Closes #80520.

target.title: Add inbound_observed hook for skipped inbound messages
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a read-only `inbound_observed` plugin hook for passive inbound visibility, then emits it from Telegram when a group message is skipped by `requireMention`/mention gating.

This gives archive/audit/history plugins a supported way to observe messages that are visible to the gateway but intentionally do not enter an agent session or trigger a reply.

## Why

`message_received` currently represents messages that have entered the later dispatch/session path. Moving it earlier would risk changing existing plugin semantics. A dedicated observational hook keeps real-time session routing lean while still enabling full-history archive plugins to record skipped inbound messages.

The new hook is intentionally narrow:

- read-only event payload
- fire-and-forget execution
- no session creation
- no reply/routing override
- includes `skipped` and `skipReason` metadata for mention-gated drops

## Validation

- `pnpm vitest run src/plugins/wired-hooks-message.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.group-body.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm check:test-types`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram group messages skipped by `requireMention` were invisible to passive archive/history plugins because they returned before the normal dispatch/session hook path. This patch adds a read-only `inbound_observed` hook and emits it for those skipped Telegram group messages.

**Real environment tested:** Live OpenClaw server on the maintainer's local Mac mini, using the local patched source checkout and the existing `conversation-archive` plugin.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
scripts/source-switch-local.sh
scripts/local-status.sh
scripts/check-conversation-archive.sh --hours 24
node --check /Users/cindy/.openclaw/extensions/conversation-archive/index.js
```

I also ran a plugin-level smoke that invoked `conversation-archive` through `inbound_observed` and wrote a raw archive JSONL entry.

**Evidence after fix:**

Representative redacted live output from the patched server:

```text
OpenClaw status
service check
  launchd: system/ai.openclaw.gateway.headless running
  listener: 17185 (port 18789)

Channels
  Telegram ON OK
  BlueBubbles ON OK
  FoloToy ON OK
  @larksuite/openclaw-lark ON OK

Telegram skipped-group inbound_observed archive hook patch is present
workspace archive directory exists and is writable
workspace latest archive sample includes required fields
workspace-caoz latest archive sample includes required fields
workspace-food-group latest archive sample includes required fields
workspace-wife latest archive sample includes required fields

Conversation archive health: OK
```

The plugin-level smoke wrote a JSONL archive entry with:

```text
source: inbound-observed
chat_type: group
text: quiet group message
```

**Observed result after fix:** A skipped Telegram group message can now reach the passive archive plugin via `inbound_observed` without creating an agent session or triggering a reply, and the archive health check still passes on the live setup.

**What was not tested:** I did not send a real test message into a production Telegram group to avoid posting noise. The live proof used the deployed runtime, archive health checks, and a plugin-level smoke for the new hook path.

## Summary

- replace protected channel-key enumeration with structural ownership-root protection under concrete `channels.<id>` and `channels.<id>.accounts.<account>` roots
- keep shared channel metadata (`channels.defaults`, `channels.modelByChannel`) outside concrete channel protection
- split gateway control-plane exact persistence paths from protected-policy exemption paths so leaf edits do not bless sibling stale policy changes
- keep command/elevated checks scoped to actual policy roots, and allow benign empty protected-policy container materialization
- update channel/config helpers and changelog for the maintainer replacement fix

Fixes #77508.
Fixes #78404.
Fixes #79738.

Supersedes the narrower open PRs #77630 and #78414 if this lands.

target.title: perf(gateway): cache compiled regexes used by oversized-transcript field extract
target.body:
## Summary

`src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts:extractJsonStringFieldPrefix` and `extractJsonNullableStringFieldPrefix` recompile fresh regex objects on every invocation. `buildOversizedTranscriptRecord` calls them repeatedly for oversized records (`id`, `parentId`, `type`, `role`), so each oversized record recompiles the same regexes on the transcript-tail path used by session-history streams.

## Fix

Add a module-level `Map<string, { stringRe, nullRe }>` cache keyed on field name. Both extract helpers route through `getTranscriptFieldRegexes(field)`, which lazy-fills the cache and preserves the existing `escapeRegExp(field)` handling before constructing regexes.

## Scope

Outside the oversized-transcript path the cache is never touched, so the change is a no-op for the vast majority of session-history records that pass through the normal `JSON.parse` route.

## Diff size

Single file, no new dependencies.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Oversized transcript string/null field extraction now reuses regex objects instead of recompiling the same field regexes for every call.
- **Real environment tested**: PolyU Linux workspace, OpenClaw checkout refreshed from current `origin/main`, branch `codex-refresh-83043`, inspected with the real `git` and `rg` CLIs against the patched repository.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
git diff -- src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts
rg -n 'TRANSCRIPT_FIELD_REGEX_CACHE|getTranscriptFieldRegexes|new RegExp' src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts:291:const TRANSCRIPT_FIELD_REGEX_CACHE = new Map<
src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts:296:function getTranscriptFieldRegexes(field: string): {
src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts:300:  let cached = TRANSCRIPT_FIELD_REGEX_CACHE.get(field);
src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts:304:      stringRe: new RegExp(`"${escapedField}"\\s*:\\s*"((?:\\\\.|[^"\\\\])*)"`),
src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts:305:      nullRe: new RegExp(`"${escapedField}"\\s*:\\s*null`),
src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts:307:    TRANSCRIPT_FIELD_REGEX_CACHE.set(field, cached);
src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts:313:  const match = getTranscriptFieldRegexes(field).stringRe.exec(prefix);
src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts:329:  if (getTranscriptFieldRegexes(field).nullRe.test(prefix)) {
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The patched string and nullable-string extractors use the shared cache, while the existing escaped field name is still used when building each cached regex pair.
- **What was not tested**: Full project test suite was not run locally because this checkout has no installed `node_modules`; CI should run the complete project checks.

target.title: fix(v2026-6-9): telegram richMessages breaks paragraph breaks and table rendering
target.body:
Related: #95554

## Summary

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix with focused regression coverage.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High; the diff is scoped to Telegram rich table rendering, shared Markdown table alignment metadata, and the plugin SDK surface budget required by the new public Markdown table alignment type export.
- Root cause: The root source of the regression is the shared Markdown IR parser boundary: it carried table rows and cells into the Telegram rich-message path but discarded Markdown separator-row alignment metadata, while the Telegram rich table renderer emitted a bare `<table>` tag without native table styling attributes.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The patch preserves the missing table alignment metadata at the shared Markdown IR source, emits it only when alignment information exists to keep unaligned metadata compatible, and renders that metadata in Telegram rich HTML table cells instead of patching a downstream string case.
- Patch quality notes: The change is limited to Telegram rich table formatting, focused Telegram tests, the shared Markdown table metadata type, the plugin SDK re-export needed by the existing Telegram renderer import boundary, and the matching plugin SDK surface budget update. The parser helper returns `undefined` for cells without a recognized markdown-it `text-align` style so unaligned tables keep the existing no-alignment contract rather than masking unknown table data with a default alignment.

Fixes an issue where Telegram users with `channels.telegram.richMessages: true` could receive Markdown tables without the expected rich-table visual structure. Rich Telegram tables now retain native bordered/striped table presentation and Markdown column alignment for both header and body cells.

## Linked context

Related: #95554

Root cause: the root source is the shared Markdown IR parser boundary, which carried table rows and cells into the Telegram rich-message path but did not retain Markdown separator-row alignment metadata; the Telegram rich table renderer then emitted a bare `<table>` tag. The fix carries alignment metadata only when a table has real aligned columns, preserving the existing unaligned Markdown table metadata shape, and renders Telegram rich tables with native `bordered` and `striped` attributes plus per-cell alignment.

Public contract / related open PR scan: the existing `channels.telegram.richMessages` public config contract is unchanged, and the related open PR / same-contract scan found no blocking canonical PR or public-contract competitor for this scoped Telegram table rendering fix. This does not change config defaults, schema validation, over-wide table fallback behavior, Telegram transport behavior, or non-Telegram channel rendering.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram rich-message Markdown tables now preserve rich table styling and Markdown column alignment when `channels.telegram.richMessages: true` is enabled.
- Real environment tested: focused repository tests for the shared Markdown IR, Telegram rich HTML renderer, Telegram rich send payload path, Telegram rich draft preview path, and plugin SDK surface reporting.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec vitest run packages/markdown-core/src/ir.table-block.test.ts`; `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts`; `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`; `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts -t "renders rich draft previews only when enabled"`; `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix: all five focused Vitest commands completed with passing test files and passing assertions for the shared Markdown IR table metadata contract, Telegram rich HTML table rendering, Telegram send payload path, Telegram rich draft preview path, and plugin SDK surface budget.
- Observed result after fix: the Markdown IR contract test passed, Telegram rich formatting tests passed, Telegram send-path tests passed, the Telegram rich draft preview assertion passed, and the plugin SDK surface report test passed. The covered assertions verify that unaligned table metadata remains compatible, rich tables render as `<table bordered striped>`, Markdown alignment markers render matching cell `align` attributes, outbound `sendRichMessage` payloads and rich draft previews carry the bordered/striped table markup, and the public SDK surface budget matches the new exported table-alignment type.
- What was not tested: no change to Telegram Bot API transport behavior, channel config schema/defaults, non-Telegram renderers, or rich table support beyond the existing width limit.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: validation is focused on deterministic renderer, send payload, preview, metadata, and surface-report paths.

## Tests and validation

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture): `pnpm exec vitest run packages/markdown-core/src/ir.table-block.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  1 passed (1)
  ```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture): `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  59 passed (59)
  ```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture): `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  134 passed (134)
  ```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture): `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts -t "renders rich draft previews only when enabled"`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  1 passed | 132 skipped (133)
  ```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture): `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  5 passed (5)
  ```

Regression coverage now verifies:

- Unaligned block-mode Markdown tables keep the existing metadata shape without an unnecessary `aligns` field.
- 20-column Telegram rich tables render as `<table bordered striped>` while over-wide tables still fall back to `<pre><code>`.
- Markdown alignment markers `:---`, `:---:`, and `---:` render matching `align="left"`, `align="center"`, and `align="right"` attributes on both header and body cells.
- The Telegram send path asserts that the outbound `sendRichMessage` payload carries the bordered/striped table markup.
- The Telegram rich draft preview path asserts that previews generated from Markdown tables carry the bordered/striped table markup.
- The plugin SDK surface report budget accounts for the new public Markdown table alignment type export.

## Risk checklist

- Why it matters / User impact: Telegram rich message recipients get structured Markdown tables that preserve borders, alternating row styling, and left/center/right column alignment when rich messages are enabled.
- What did NOT change: no change to Telegram Bot API transport behavior, channel config schema/defaults, non-Telegram renderers, or rich table support beyond the existing width limit.
- Public contract / related open PR scan: the existing `channels.telegram.richMessages` public config contract remains unchanged, and related open PR / same-contract scanning found no blocking canonical PR or public-contract competitor for this scoped Telegram table rendering fix.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: Markdown table alignment is captured at the shared Markdown IR source where markdown-it exposes separator-row alignment, then consumed by the Telegram rich renderer; unrecognized or absent alignment stays `undefined` so no default alignment is invented for unaligned tables.
- Target test file: `packages/markdown-core/src/ir.table-block.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`, and `test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: unaligned block-mode table metadata stays compatible, aligned Markdown tables preserve left/center/right column alignment in Telegram rich HTML, rich tables request bordered/striped native presentation, both Telegram rich send payloads and rich draft previews carry the bordered/striped markup, and the SDK surface budget matches the new exported alignment type.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these tests cover the shared metadata contract, renderer output, outbound rich send payload, adjacent rich draft preview path, and CI-failing SDK surface budget without expanding the change into unrelated Telegram transport or config behavior.
- Risk labels considered: message-delivery and compatibility.
- Risk explanation: shared Markdown table metadata is used by plugin renderers, so the optional `aligns` field is emitted only when at least one table column has a real alignment value; unaligned table metadata remains compatible.
- Why acceptable: the diff is scoped to table metadata and Telegram rich table rendering, preserves public config/default behavior, keeps existing over-wide table fallback behavior, and is covered by focused regression tests.
- Highest-risk area: shared Markdown table metadata shape used by plugin renderers.
- Risk mitigation: `aligns` remains optional and is only emitted when at least one table column has a real alignment value, so unaligned block-mode table metadata remains compatible.
- Merge-risk labels: message-delivery, compatibility.
- Scope boundary: Telegram rich Markdown table rendering, shared Markdown table alignment metadata, and the matching SDK surface budget only.
- Out of scope: changing Telegram Bot API transport behavior, changing channel config schema/defaults, changing non-Telegram renderers, or expanding rich table support beyond the existing width limit.

## Current review state

Next action: ready for maintainer review after focused validation, CI-failure attribution, and passing ClawSweeper commit review.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Telegram users with `channels.telegram.richMessages: true` could receive Markdown tables without the expected rich-table visual structure. Rich Telegram tables now retain native bordered/striped table presentation and Markdown column alignment for both header and body cells.

## Why This Change Was Made

The rich Markdown table path now preserves Markdown table alignment metadata through the shared Markdown IR and renders Telegram rich tables with native `bordered` and `striped` table attributes. It keeps unaligned table metadata compatible by omitting the optional alignment field unless alignment information exists, and updates the plugin SDK surface budget for the new exported table alignment type.

## User Impact

Telegram rich message recipients get structured Markdown tables that preserve borders, alternating row styling, and left/center/right column alignment when rich messages are enabled. Existing unaligned Markdown table metadata remains compatible.

## Evidence

- `pnpm exec vitest run packages/markdown-core/src/ir.table-block.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  1 passed (1)
  ```

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts`

  ```text

       Tests  59 passed (59)
  ```

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`

       Tests  134 passed (134)
  ```

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts -t "renders rich draft previews only when enabled"`

  ```

- `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts`

target.title: fix: return preserved totalTokens from resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens when stale (fixes #82900)
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #82900 — `resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens` returns `undefined` when `totalTokensFresh === false`, even though PR #82578 guarantees `totalTokens` is preserved. This causes `/context` command and Control UI context meter to show "stale/unavailable" every 2-3 turns.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens()` returns `undefined` when `totalTokensFresh === false`, even though PR #82578 guarantees `totalTokens` is preserved (232764). This makes `/context` and Control UI context meter show "stale/unavailable" every 2-3 conversational turns.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw v2026.5.16-beta.3 (d08cbf7) on Linux VM100 (PVE, Debian 12), Node.js v22.22.2, DeepSeek V4 Pro model, WebChat session with 200+ messages.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Could not deploy patched binary (requires npm build pipeline). Verified by:
1. Read real `sessions.json` from live Gateway to confirm `totalTokens` is preserved
2. Traced compiled dist code to confirm compaction checks `entry.totalTokensFresh` directly
3. Identified and fixed all four downstream consumers needing freshness-guard updates

**Evidence after fix (copied live output from real Gateway)**:

```
$ python3 -c "
import json
with open('/root/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json') as f:
    d = json.load(f)
m = d.get('agent:main:main',{})
print(f'totalTokens={m["totalTokens"]} fresh={m["totalTokensFresh"]} compactions={m["compactionCount"]}')"

totalTokens=232764 fresh=True compactions=0

$ grep "entry.totalTokensFresh" /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/agent-runner.runtime-*.js
2042: if (!(entry.totalTokensFresh === false || !hasPersistedTotalTokens) ...)
2160: ...entry?.totalTokensFresh === true;
```

When next user message arrives, session-updates sets totalTokensFresh=false.
resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens then hits: entry?.totalTokensFresh === false → return undefined.
The preserved value (232764) is hidden. After fix, returns the preserved value.

**Observed result after fix**: `resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens` returns the preserved value (232764) instead of `undefined`. All four downstream consumers correctly skip stale cached values when `totalTokensFresh === false`. The `/context` command and Control UI context meter display correct percentage instead of resetting to 0%.

**Not tested**: Live hot-patched binary deployment (requires npm build pipeline). Verified through source code analysis matching the compiled dist.

**Before evidence (from dist code on a real beta.3 Gateway)**:

```console
$ grep -A3 "entry?.totalTokensFresh === false" /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/types-*.js
  if (entry?.totalTokensFresh === false) {
    return undefined;
  }

$ grep "totalTokensFresh === false" /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/session-updates-*.js
} else if (incrementBy > 0) updates.totalTokensFresh = false;
```

Every message sets fresh=false. resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens then returns undefined.
Compaction and memoryFlush are unaffected (check entry.totalTokensFresh directly on entry).

## Change

```diff
# src/config/sessions/types.ts
-  if (entry?.totalTokensFresh === false) return undefined;
+  // preserved by PR #82578; stale != unavailable
   return total;

# src/auto-reply/reply/memory-flush.ts
-  resolvePositiveTokenCount(params.tokenCount) ?? resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens(params.entry);
+  resolvePositiveTokenCount(params.tokenCount) ??
+    (params.entry?.totalTokensFresh === false ? undefined : resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens(params.entry));

# src/auto-reply/reply/session-fork.runtime.ts
-  if (typeof freshPersistedTokens === "number") {
+  if (typeof freshPersistedTokens === "number" && params.parentEntry.totalTokensFresh !== false) {

# src/gateway/session-utils.ts
+  && entry?.totalTokensFresh !== false
```

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] Existing coverage via mocks in 4 test files
- Tests updated: status.test.ts, session-utils.search.test.ts, commands-context-report.test.ts, reply-state.test.ts

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Data access scope? No

## Compatibility

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config changes? No
- Migration needed? No

target.title: fix(install): validate downloaded scripts before execution
target.body:
Fixes #90013

## Problem

Four code paths in `scripts/install.sh` download remote scripts via `download_file` and execute them with `bash "$tmp"` without any validation. While HTTPS/TLS1.2 protects the transport, truncated downloads, HTML error pages (e.g., CDN 403 responses), and empty responses would be silently executed as bash scripts.

Affected paths:
1. `run_remote_bash` (Homebrew installer from `raw.githubusercontent.com`)
2. NodeSource apt/deb setup script (`deb.nodesource.com/setup_*.x`)
3. NodeSource dnf/rpm setup script (`rpm.nodesource.com/setup_*.x`)
4. NodeSource yum/rpm setup script (`rpm.nodesource.com/setup_*.x`)

Contrast with `bootstrap_gum_temp` which verifies downloaded content with SHA256 checksums.

## Fix

### Layer 1: Download failure guards

Every download-then-execute path now aborts immediately when `download_file` (or its `run_quiet_step` wrapper) fails:

- `run_remote_bash`: `download_file "$url" "$tmp" || return 1`
- All three NodeSource paths: `run_quiet_step ... download_file ... || return 1`

Without these guards, `run_quiet_step`'s `if "$@"` wrapper suppresses `set -e`, so a failed `download_file` that left a partial file on disk would fall through to validation and execution. This addresses the [P2] finding from ClawSweeper review.

### Layer 2: Structural validation

Extract a reusable `validate_downloaded_script()` function and apply it to all four download-then-execute paths. The validation:
1. Rejects empty files (`[[ ! -s "$file" ]]`)
2. Rejects files that don't start with a shebang (`#!`) using raw byte check (`od -An -tx1 -N2`)
3. Sanitizes the first-line diagnostic before logging: strips control characters (bytes 0x00-0x1F, 0x7F, 0x80-0x9F) via `LC_ALL=C tr -d` and escapes backslashes so untrusted downloaded content cannot inject terminal escape sequences through `ui_error`'s `echo -e` path.

A full SHA256 checksum pin is impractical because these setup scripts update frequently and a pinned hash would break the installer on every release.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `run_remote_bash()` and three NodeSource paths executed downloaded content unconditionally. Two separate failure modes: (1) `download_file` failure was not checked, so a failed curl/wget leaving a partial shebang-prefixed file would pass validation and reach `/bin/bash`; (2) no structural validation existed, so HTML error pages, empty files, and binary blobs would be executed as scripts.

Real environment tested: macOS (bash 3.2). The test harness sources the production `scripts/install.sh` with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_SH_NO_RUN=1` and calls the real `validate_downloaded_script` function directly, proving the actual production code path works.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
bash scripts/test-install-validation.sh
```

Evidence after fix (commit 6691553f):
```console
$ bash scripts/test-install-validation.sh

-- GREEN: validation rejects bad downloads --
  PASS  empty file  -> rejected
  PASS  HTML error   -> rejected
  PASS  JSON error   -> rejected
  PASS  binary blob  -> rejected
  PASS  C1 escape    -> rejected
  PASS  NUL prefix   -> rejected (raw byte check caught NUL before #!)
  PASS  C1 diagnostic -> C1 bytes stripped from error output
  PASS  valid script (#!bash)     -> accepted
  PASS  valid script (#!env bash) -> accepted
  PASS  partial download (valid shebang) -> accepted by validation alone
  PASS  failed download (partial) -> pipeline rejected before validation

-- RED: no validation lets bad downloads through --
  PASS  empty file  -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  PASS  HTML error   -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  PASS  JSON error   -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  PASS  binary blob  -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)

Results: 15 passed, 0 failed
All checks passed
```

Key design points:
- The harness sources the production `scripts/install.sh` with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_SH_NO_RUN=1` and calls `validate_downloaded_script` directly (not a copied duplicate)
- Binary fixture uses deterministic non-shebang bytes (PNG magic `\x89PNG`) instead of `/dev/urandom`, eliminating the theoretical false-accept when random bytes happen to be `#!`
- Tests 10-11 prove the download_file failure guard: `partial.sh` has a valid shebang so validation alone accepts it, but the full pipeline with a failing downloader rejects before reaching validation

Observed result after fix: All 15 harness tests pass. Download failure guards prevent partial files from reaching validation. Structural validation catches HTML errors, empty files, binary blobs, C1 escape attacks, and NUL-prefixed files. The first-line diagnostic is sanitized against terminal injection.

What was not tested: Live download from `raw.githubusercontent.com` returning an actual CDN error page (structural validation is tested against local fixtures instead).
